# Anton Kundenko

> Principal systems engineer and architect of high-performance database systems. Creator of the Rayfall and O programming languages. 18+ years building performance-critical infrastructure in C and custom runtimes.

Anton Kundenko designs and ships columnar analytical databases and the languages that drive them. Currently at Lynx Trading Technologies (2013-present), where his systems have replaced kdb+/q across hundreds of production instances. Earlier at Samsung Electronics R&D (2012-2013), porting KVM to ARM Cortex-A with TrustZone integration.

His work focuses on SIMD-first execution, zero-allocation hot paths, morsel-driven query scheduling, and the language surfaces that expose those engines to analysts.

## Projects

- [RayforceDB](https://rayforcedb.com): SIMD-first columnar analytical database in C with the Rayfall language. Sponsored by Lynx; replaces kdb+/q in production.
- [ThePlatform](https://theplatform.technology): Columnar time-series database and the O language. Internal at Lynx.
- [TeideDB](https://teidedb.com): Analytical SQL surface over the Rayforce vectorized core.
- [Teidelum](https://lum.teidedb.com): Open-source knowledge infrastructure on Teide, exposing data via MCP and REST.
- [AxlDB](https://axl-db.com): Ultra-compact zero-dependency vector database for deterministic-latency workloads.
- [hyperbridge](https://github.com/singaraiona/hyperbridge): Open-source Rust crate for fast multi-producer, multi-consumer unbounded channels.

## Writing

Technical blog at https://anton.kundenko.dev/writing covering columnar engine internals, SIMD and allocator design, programming language theory (k, APL lineage), morsel-driven execution, datalog on analytical engines, query scheduling, and systems debugging.

- Index: https://anton.kundenko.dev/writing
- RSS feed: https://anton.kundenko.dev/rss.xml
- Career map: https://anton.kundenko.dev/career
